title: Close out Phase 2: epic retro, then flesh out Phase 3 (zk7) into ticks
description:
The close-out task per the roadmap convention (skills/ticks/SKILL.md, Roadmaps section).

1. Final review of the epic's full diff: internal/query/plan.go, cmd/tk/cmd/next.go, cmd/tk/cmd/graph.go, activity reader + list.go — consistency of the 'action'/'needs_planning' additive JSON fields, no behavior change for existing shapes, go test ./... green.
2. Run the epic-close retro (skills/ticks/references/claude-runner.md, Epic-close retro): harvest tick notes + agent reports, promote by tier, compact .tick/learnings.md (150-line cap), outside-in verification of phase 2 scope against the code, drift review of the diff, retro report as the epic close reason.
3. Flesh out Phase 3 (epic zk7 — meta-work-as-ticks + re-entrant orchestration loop) into ticks per its rough scope, using the foundation-first partitioning procedure (skills/ticks/references/tick-patterns.md) and re-reading .tick/learnings.md first.

zk7 is NOT gated, so per the two-tier stopping rule execution flows on into Phase 3 unless the human says otherwise.
acceptance_criteria: Epic diff reviewed and retro report written as close reason; .tick/learnings.md compacted; zk7 has child ticks incl. its own close-out task
closed_reason: Close-out executed: reviewer subagent found 1 blocker + 3 should-fix + 3 nits, all fixed by original implementers and merged; outside-in verification dogfooded on this repo's live ticks; Phase 3 (zk7) fleshed into 4 ticks (3 waves)
